LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS LISA NASH AND PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS 453038 AND 462874. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE COURT OF APPEALS HAS MADE CLEAR THAT HARTFORD "CAN RECOVER AGAINST THE SETTLEMENT AND IS NOT REQUIRED TO PURSUE A DIRECT ACTION AGAINST A THIRD PARTY" (HARTFORD I AT 7). THAT IS WHAT HARTFORD II IS ABOUT AND THERE IS NO REASON TO DELAY THAT TRIAL. ALTHOUGH BOTH CASES INVOLVE THE SAME UNDERLYING EVENT, IT SEEMS CLEAR THAT THE EVIDENCE TO BE OFFERED BY HARTFORD IN HARTFORD II IS LIMITED AS CONTRASTED WITH THE NEED TO ESTABLISH PG&E'S NEGLIGENCE IN HARTFORD I. MOREOVER, THERE IS A REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT THE RESULT IN HARTFORD II WILL MOOT THE NEED TO TRY HARTFORD I. PLAINTIFF AS PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

calendar.matters:
Defendants' Motion To Consolidate Actions 453038 And 462874
Off Calendar Nov-26-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendants Lisa Nash and Pacific Gas & Electric's Motion To Consolidate Actions 453038 And 462874.
APPEARANCES: Thomas C. Tagliarini, Esq. of Law Offices of Thomas C. Tagliarini 510/444-0692 for pltf & opposing party Hartford Accident & Indemnity Company; Nina G. Shapirshteyn, Esq. of O'Reilly & Danko 650/358-5901 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE COURT OF APPEALS HAS MADE CLEAR THAT HARTFORD "CAN RECOVER AGAINST THE SETTLEMENT AND IS NOT REQUIRED TO PURSUE A DIRECT ACTION AGAINST A THIRD PARTY" (HARTFORD I AT 7). THAT IS WHAT HARTFORD II IS ABOUT AND THERE IS NO REASON TO DELAY THAT TRIAL. ALTHOUGH BOTH CASES INVOLVE THE SAME UNDERLYING EVENT, IT SEEMS CLEAR THAT THE EVIDENCE TO BE OFFERED BY HARTFORD IN HARTFORD II IS LIMITED AS CONTRASTED WITH THE NEED TO ESTABLISH PG&E'S NEGLIGENCE IN HARTFORD I. MOREOVER, THERE IS A REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT THE RESULT IN HARTFORD II WILL MOOT THE NEED TO TRY HARTFORD I. Plaintiff as prevailing party to prepare proposed form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
